response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but misses several hard requirements: there is no indication of native-level Japanese (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and only brief C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, ~7 months) rather than strong C for UNIX development. Critically, there is no background in L2/L3 networking, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), embedded switch firmware, or chip-vendor SDKs. The mismatch on mandatory qualifications makes this a poor fit despite solid general engineering ability.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship)
Low-latency systems experience with async I/O and high-throughput services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
No demonstrated Linux kernel analysis/modification or strong C-for-UNIX experience
No networking security (MACsec, 802.1X), LAN-switch, or embedded firmware product background

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering and testing discipline
